content.full_text:
CHIEF JUSTICE
MALCOLM M LUCAS
CHAIRPERSON

JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA

ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE OF THE COURTS
WILLIAM E DAVIS
DIRECTOR

303 2ND STREET, SOUTH TOWER
SAN FRANCISCO 94107 • (415) 396-9100

LEGISLATIVE OFFICE
801 K STREET, SUITE 1800, SACRAMENTO 95814 • (916) 445-7524

March 29, 1991

Mr. John Mitchell
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA    95804

Dear Mr. Mitchell:

I am requesting confirmation of the phone advice given to me by you
and by Jean Pritchard on March 26, 1991 and March 28, 1991
regarding my disclosure responsibilities with respect to my
position as manager of the Family Court Services unit at the
Administrative Office of the Courts and my business and income to
the business.

The disclosure category that applies to my position reads:
"Category 12.   Investments and positions* in and income from
business entities that are providers** of related family court
services(including consulting and training) and related equipment
and supplies." *(Positions include employee and partner, officer,
director, trustee, and any other management position.)
**(Providers include business entities and individuals that are
manufacturers, distributors, vendors, sellers, leasors, suppliers,
contractors, subcontractors, and other providers of the supplies,
equipment, real property, and services indicated in this category.)

The business is a sole proprietorship and was formed in 1980 under
the title, "The New Family Center". Since that time it has been
treated as a separate business entity, with appropriate business
expenses, and deductions each year. The nature of the business is
that of providing consulting, educational, and counseling services
including written materials with some appearances on a panels or as
a speaker for professional associations in the area of family law,
family mediation, individual and couple counseling, divorce, and
custody. Speechmaking is not the principle nature of the business.
The sources of the income to this business are outside of the state
of California and these sources do not do business with the AOC
unit. Income to this business does not exceed $10,000. from a
single source.

My understanding is that unless the income to this business from a
single source is greater than $10,000., it is not to be disclosed.

Further, it is my understanding that the honoraria and gift limits
effective Jan. 1, 1991 would not apply to this business as long as
the work was done in connection with this bone fide business and
the predominant activity was not that of making speeches.
Therefore my statement on Form 730 consists of Schedule A and
Schedule D. I have filed these statements with the AOC Personnel
unit which is responsible for forwarding them to your office.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Isolina Ricci, Ph.D.

sections.question_synthetic: The sender is requesting written confirmation of prior phone advice regarding disclosure obligations for a position managing Family Court Services, specifically whether business income from a sole proprietorship providing consulting and counseling services must be disclosed under Category L2, and whether honoraria/gift limits apply.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a member of the public to FPPC, not an FPPC response/advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. Document references prior phone advice but seeks written confirmation. Sender describes their understanding of disclosure requirements rather than asking a new question.

embedding.summary: This is a letter from an AOC employee to FPPC requesting written confirmation of phone advice previously given regarding disclosure responsibilities for a business income from a sole proprietorship providing family law consulting and counseling services, with the understanding that income under $10,000 from a single source need not be disclosed.
